Ever noticed your Matrice 4 Dock version drone returning to DJI Dock 3 even when the battery doesn’t appear low? That’s intentional. DJI’s R
snippet: Ever noticed your Matrice 4 Dock version drone returning to DJI Dock 3 even when the battery doesn’t appear low? That’s intentional. DJI’s Return-to-Home (RTH) system uses a smart algorithm that evaluates multiple flight parameters before deciding when the aircraft should return. The calculation considers: 📍 Remaining battery capacity 📍 Distance from the Dock 📍 Flight altitude 📍 Real-time wind conditions 📍 Built-in 15% safety buffer This ensures the aircraft always has enough power to safely reach DJI Dock 3, even if conditions change during the mission. When Low-Battery RTH activates, the safest option is to allow the aircraft to land automatically. In strong winds or unstable weather, operators may also consider returning at around 50% battery for additional safety. Smart battery logic is one of the key factors that makes automated dock operations reliable and scalable. DJI Dock 3 allows drone missions to run as a complete automated cycle, from take off through to landing and reset. Once a flight is complete
snippet: DJI Dock 3 allows drone missions to run as a complete automated cycle, from take off through to landing and reset. Once a flight is complete, the Matrice 4TD returns to the dock, aligns with the landing platform and lands precisely before the dock closes and the aircraft begins charging ready for the next deployment. The entire process runs automatically, ensuring the system is ready whenever the next mission is required. For organisations introducing autonomous drone capability, reliability at this stage is essential. The ability for the aircraft to return, land and prepare itself for the next flight ensures the system can continue operating without manual intervention. For infrastructure, utilities and industrial environments, this is what allows drone operations to move beyond one off deployments and become part of routine operational oversight. title: Last week was one of those weeks that really reminds you how far this industry has come. In just five days, our team deployed seven DJI Dock
snippet: Last week was one of those weeks that really reminds you how far this industry has come. In just five days, our team deployed seven DJI Dock systems across three different states, supporting ongoing renewable energy and infrastructure projects that rely on consistent, high-quality geospatial data. Deployments like this go far beyond simply placing hardware in the field. Each site requires careful planning, site surveys, connectivity setup, system testing, and power integration to ensure the docks are ready to operate safely and reliably from day one. Once operational, these systems enable automated daily aerial data collection, providing construction teams with a consistent, real-time view of progress across large and rapidly evolving job sites. Many of these deployments are also fully off-grid, which adds another layer of complexity. That’s where strong partnerships make all the difference. snippet: We are excited to reveal our Team UAV truck, equipped with DJI Dock 3, enabling us to deploy fully autonomous aerial capability wherever it is needed. This positions Team UAV as one of the first operators in the UK to deploy Dock 3 as a mobile platform. Dock 3 houses, charges and deploys the aircraft automatically. Integrated onto the truck, it can be transported directly into live environments and activated without traditional mobilisation. Flights can be scheduled, triggered on demand and operated remotely. For our clients, this means faster deployment, consistent data capture and reduced operational disruption across live environments. Fixed autonomy is powerful. Mobile autonomy takes it further. The Team UAV truck with Dock 3 is now operational. If you would like more information or a live demonstration to see how this could support your operations, please get in touch with our team.

title: “DJI Dock 3: When Winter Freezes, Your Drone Ops Don’t” “Snow, ice, sub‑zero wind… and your drone system is still online.” DJI Dock 3 is bui
snippet: “DJI Dock 3: When Winter Freezes, Your Drone Ops Don’t” “Snow, ice, sub‑zero wind… and your drone system is still online.” DJI Dock 3 is built to keep autonomous drone operations running in harsh environments, not just sunny days. In this alpine deployment, the dock sits directly in the cold – its housing covered in frost – yet it continues to shelter, charge and manage the drone for fully automated missions. That makes it ideal for: Mountain and valley infrastructure inspection (power lines, towers, cable cars, roads). Remote energy sites and pipelines in cold or hard‑to‑reach terrain. Safety and environmental monitoring above snow‑covered slopes and valleys. With DJI Dock 3, teams can launch drones on‑demand or on schedule without sending staff into the elements, getting live video and data from the control room instead. XDRON helps organisations design, deploy and support DJI Dock 3 solutions for rugged, all‑weather operations. hashtag #DJIDock3 hashtag #DroneInABox hashtag #Autonom

🚀 This week, we had the opportunity to test Nokia's fully autonomous drone system, designed and manufactured entirely in-house! 🚁 The dron
snippet: 🚀 This week, we had the opportunity to test Nokia's fully autonomous drone system, designed and manufactured entirely in-house! 🚁 The drone operates from automated docking stations that are climate-controlled, automatically position the drone upon landing, and recharge it for the next mission. With a payload capacity of up to 3 kg, the drone is versatile for various applications. 📸 Equipped with high-resolution visible spectrum and infrared cameras, the drone can also be fitted with alternative payloads such as network measurement devices or scanners. The modular design allows for custom adapters, enabling the integration of diverse equipment as per mission requirements. 🪂 Safety is paramount with features like an emergency parachute system and dual integrated modems. These modems ensure continuous connectivity to both private and public networks, maintaining a constant link to the control center. 🌍 Real-world deployments underscore the system's efficacy: 👉 In Belgium, hashtag #C

🚀 Russia Unveils First Autonomous Orbital Drone Station 🎛️ Russia has just patented a groundbreaking space innovation: an orbital station
snippet: 🚀 Russia Unveils First Autonomous Orbital Drone Station 🎛️ Russia has just patented a groundbreaking space innovation: an orbital station capable of automatically launching spacecraft and using robotic drones for self-maintenance, a global first. 🛰️🤖 The system is scheduled for testing on the upcoming Russian Orbital Station (ROS) and is being considered for the country’s lunar missions . This tech leap could redefine space operations, enabling robot-led repairs and resupply without needing human crews onboard. Testing is expected to begin around 2027, with full deployment aimed for completion by 2030–2033. What a spectacular stride in autonomous orbital engineering, a true game‑changer for efficient, low‑risk space missions! 🌌✨

snippet: Somewear Labs is heading to the Tough Stump Technologies Rodeo next week, and we’re looking forward to pushing the boundaries of tactical communication and demonstrating the power of our software-defined network. We're excited to showcase: -- MANET Resilience -- See how Somewear's BLOS narrowband backhaul seamlessly extends your tactical reach. When your MANET link breaks, Somewear’s SmartRouting™ logic takes over, providing critical BLOS connectivity for data to continue flowing across all linked MANETs and also back to command. We'll demonstrate this capability with radios from Silvus Technologies, TrellisWare Technologies, and Doodle Labs. -- Unmanned Operations -- Discover how our low SWaP-C (size, weight, power, and cost) Node provides LOS and BLOS control and enhanced situational awareness for UxS operations. Range is no longer a limitation for mission commands and data flow. -- Networking UAV Wireless Charging Stations -- We'll be highlighting our integration with Quaze Technolo

Excited to share that AECA Solutions is now an authorized reseller of the full suite of hashtag #DJI Enterprise hashtag #drone solutions in
snippet: Excited to share that AECA Solutions is now an authorized reseller of the full suite of hashtag #DJI Enterprise hashtag #drone solutions in Malaysia — including the revolutionary hashtag #DJIDock3, a fully autonomous drone docking station. This development marks an exciting new chapter in our drone portfolio. After several successful years as the authorized distributor of hashtag #QuantumSystems’ eVTOL hashtag #fixedwingdrones — now the market leader in Malaysia — the DJI partnership allows us to further expand our offerings. title: Late post: I'm happy to share a look at a project delivered at the end of last year in Sumedang City. While the implementation was completed
snippet: Late post: I'm happy to share a look at a project delivered at the end of last year in Sumedang City. While the implementation was completed in late 2025, the municipality officially announced it to the public earlier this month (see: https://lnkd.in/gKWcN6SH). Working as the technology partner alongside PT Alita as project owner, we aimed to consolidate smart city services into a single "Smart Pole". This is much more than a utility pole, it has: - Connectivity & Media: Micro-cell BTS, Free WiFi, LED Panel/Videotron and Public Announcer/Speaker. - Intelligence: AI-powered CCTV and an autonomous surveillance drone docking station. - Sustainability: Smart PJU lighting and EV Charging station. - Real-time Sensor Data: Environmental sensors (Air quality, temp, humidity). I think it was the first in Indonesia and will continue to evolve. I’m curious is there any similar implementations? hashtag #SmartCity hashtag #SmartPole hashtag #IoT hashtag #TechInnovation
